tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","language":"C#","stargazers_count":5975,"forks_count":5139,"open_issues_count":1324,"updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:18:52Z","created_at":"2011-12-06T23:32:47Z","topics":["azure","azure-sdk","c-sharp","dotnet","hacktoberfest","microsoft"],"private":false,"fork":false,"archived":false,"default_branch":"main"}]}

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[AgentServer] Release Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses 1.0.0-beta.3 (#58347)

* Eagerly validate previous_response_id and conversation_id before handler invocation

Force history item ID resolution from the provider before the handler runs.
This ensures invalid previous_response_id (404) or conversation_id (400)
errors surface immediately rather than being silently ignored.

The Lazy<Task<>> caching in ResponseContextImpl means handlers that later
call GetHistoryAsync() reuse the prefetched result with no extra storage call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve full Foundry storage error body (code, message, param, type) in client responses

StorageErrorMapper now extracts all structured fields from the Foundry error
JSON — code, message, param, and type — and propagates them through the
exception hierarchy to the HTTP response returned to the client.

Key changes:
- ResourceNotFoundException gains Code and Param properties (mirrors BadRequestException)
- StorageErrorMapper.ExtractErrorInfo returns (Message, Code, Param, Type) tuple
- Default (non-400/404) errors are always returned as HTTP 500, never proxying
  the upstream status code
- ResponsesApiException for catch-all now includes Param and Type from Foundry
- ApiErrorFactory.NotFound accepts optional code and param
- ResponsesExceptionFilter passes Code/Param from ResourceNotFoundException

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Prepare release: Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses 1.0.0-beta.3 (2026-05-05)

Set release date for Responses beta.3. Core and Invocations are not
released in this cycle (no changes since their last release).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: doc accuracy, guard relaxation, mock nullability

1. Updated XML doc on ThrowIfError to accurately describe which fields are
   preserved per exception type (400/404/409 → message/code/param; default →
   also type).
2. Relaxed ExtractErrorInfo guard to return parsed fields when ANY structured
   field is present (not just message). Uses generic fallback for Message.
3. Made MockResponse.Content non-nullable — uses BinaryData.FromString(body)
   even for empty bodies to match real Azure.Core.Response behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments: fix doc accuracy for conversation.id and error types

- Fix test doc: 'conversation_id' → 'conversation.id' to match protocol field name
- Expand ValidatingProvider summary to describe both 404 and 400 paths
- Update handler comment to reflect multiple possible error types (not just 404)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix changelog wording: include 409 in excluded status codes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

resource.text:
# Release History

## 1.0.0-beta.3 (2026-05-05)

### Features Added

- `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now validated against the provider before
  the handler is invoked. Invalid references return HTTP 404/400 immediately instead of being
  silently ignored. The resolved history item IDs are cached — handlers calling
  `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse the prefetched result without a second storage lookup.

### Bugs Fixed

- Foundry storage error responses now preserve the full error body (`code`, `message`, `param`, `type`)
  when returned to the client. Previously only the `message` field was forwarded; `param` was lost.
- Non-400/404/409 error status codes from Foundry storage are no longer proxied as-is; they are
  normalized to HTTP 500 to avoid leaking upstream infrastructure details.

## 1.0.0-beta.2 (2026-04-17)

### Features Added

- Added chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints. When a response is created with
  `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must
  include the same key. Mismatched or missing keys return 404 (indistinguishable from not-found)
  to ensure tenant isolation.
- Added `x-agent-session-id` response header on all protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel,
  Delete, InputItems). The resolved session ID is echoed as a response header per spec §8,
  with fallback to the `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable for error responses.
- Added validation for malformed response IDs in both the `response_id` path parameter and the
  `previous_response_id` request body field. IDs that do not match the expected format (prefix
  and length) are rejected with 400 and a descriptive error message.
- Added metadata constraint validation: metadata maps are limited to 16 keys with key length ≤ 64
  and value length ≤ 512 characters, enforced via the validator pipeline.
- Added deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation: SHA-256 hash of
  `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars.
  Falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available.
- Added eager eviction of completed `ResponseExecution` entries from the in-flight tracker,
  reducing memory pressure for long-running servers.
- Added `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` — an Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every
  outbound Foundry storage API call with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation
  headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).
- Added structured `Information`-level logging to all Responses API endpoints (GET, Cancel, Delete,
  InputItems) with response ID context. The POST `/responses` creation log now includes response ID,
  conversation ID, previous response ID, and store flag for full request traceability.
- Added isolation key presence logging (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`) to all
  endpoint handler logs and outbound Foundry storage request logs. Key values are never logged.
- Added startup configuration logging: storage provider type, default model, fetch history count,
  and event stream TTL are logged at `Information` level when the host starts.
- Added server version `User-Agent` header on all outbound Foundry storage API requests. The
  composed identity from `ServerVersionRegistry` (including developer-registered segments) is
  prepended to the standard Azure.Core user-agent, read lazily per-request.
- Added Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs: everything before `/storage` is redacted
  and query parameters are stripped (except `api-version`) to prevent leaking account and project
  information.
- Added inbound request logging for Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups (via `ResponsesServer.Run()` or
  `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`). All incoming HTTP requests are logged with method, path, status
  code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`).

### Breaking Changes

- Made `ResponsesActivitySource` internal. The activity source is managed by
  the framework; handlers do not need to create tracing activities directly.
- Made `ResponsesTracingConstants` internal. The tracing tag, baggage, and log scope
  constants are implementation details not needed by handler authors.

### Bugs Fixed

- Fixed error response shapes to match the container specification: `invalid_request_error` type
  for 400 errors and `not_found` message format.
- Fixed cancel-after-terminal to return `invalid_request_error` type with the correct error shape
  per the specification.
- Fixed DELETE endpoint to return 404 (not 400) when the response ID does not exist, aligning
  with the specification.
- Fixed cancel-after-delete to return 404 (not-found) per the specification.

## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2026-04-14)

### Features Added

- Initial release of the Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses library.
- ASP.NET Core server library implementing the Azure AI Responses API.
- `ResponseHandler` abstract class for custom response handling with `CreateAsync` returning `IAsyncEnumerable<ResponseStreamEvent>`.
- `TextResponse` convenience class for simple text-only responses with a single delegate.
- `ResponseEventStream` builder for full control over SSE event generation with automatic `sequenceNumber`, `outputIndex`, `contentIndex`, and `itemId` tracking.
- `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call:
  - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically)
  - `OutputItemMessage(string text, IEnumerable<Annotation> annotations)` — emits a text message with file annotations
  - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable<string> tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events
  - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item
  - `OutputItemFunctionCallOutput(callId, output)` — emits a function call output (no deltas)
  - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item
  - `OutputItemImageGenCall(resultBase64)` — emits an image generation result with status transitions
  - `OutputItemStructuredOutputs(output)` — emits an arbitrary structured JSON output item
  - One-liner convenience generators for all remaining simple output item types: computer calls, local shell calls, function shell calls, apply-patch calls, custom tool call outputs, MCP approval requests/responses, and compaction
- `ResponseContext` with `GetInputItemsAsync()` (returns `IReadOnlyList<Item>`) and `GetInputTextAsync()` convenience for accessing resolved input items and text content.
- `IEnumerable<Item>.GetInputText()` extension method for extracting text from any sequence of input items.
- `CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` extension for advanced access to expanded input items as `OutputItem` types.
- Built-in in-memory response provider and execution tracking.
- Support for default, streaming, background, and streaming+background response modes.
- `AgentHostBuilder` convenience methods for zero-config server startup via `ResponsesServer.Run<T>()`.
- Protocol identity registration with `ServerVersionRegistry` during route mapping.
- `x-agent-response-id` header validation matching the Responses API specification.
- Conversation ID round-trip support in both synchronous and SSE streaming modes.
- OpenTelemetry distributed tracing via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.

content.text:
{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[AgentServer] Eager history validation and storage error preservation (#58341)

* Eagerly validate previous_response_id and conversation_id before handler invocation

Force history item ID resolution from the provider before the handler runs.
This ensures invalid previous_response_id (404) or conversation_id (400)
errors surface immediately rather than being silently ignored.

The Lazy<Task<>> caching in ResponseContextImpl means handlers that later
call GetHistoryAsync() reuse the prefetched result with no extra storage call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve full Foundry storage error body (code, message, param, type) in client responses

StorageErrorMapper now extracts all structured fields from the Foundry error
JSON — code, message, param, and type — and propagates them through the
exception hierarchy to the HTTP response returned to the client.

Key changes:
- ResourceNotFoundException gains Code and Param properties (mirrors BadRequestException)
- StorageErrorMapper.ExtractErrorInfo returns (Message, Code, Param, Type) tuple
- Default (non-400/404) errors are always returned as HTTP 500, never proxying
  the upstream status code
- ResponsesApiException for catch-all now includes Param and Type from Foundry
- ApiErrorFactory.NotFound accepts optional code and param
- ResponsesExceptionFilter passes Code/Param from ResourceNotFoundException

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: doc accuracy, guard relaxation, mock nullability

1. Updated XML doc on ThrowIfError to accurately describe which fields are
   preserved per exception type (400/404/409 → message/code/param; default →
   also type).
2. Relaxed ExtractErrorInfo guard to return parsed fields when ANY structured
   field is present (not just message). Uses generic fallback for Message.
3. Made MockResponse.Content non-nullable — uses BinaryData.FromString(body)
   even for empty bodies to match real Azure.Core.Response behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments: fix doc accuracy for conversation.id and error types

- Fix test doc: 'conversation_id' → 'conversation.id' to match protocol field name
- Expand ValidatingProvider summary to describe both 404 and 400 paths
- Update handler comment to reflect multiple possible error types (not just 404)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Ravi Pidaparthi","email":"rapida@microsoft.com","date":"2026-04-20T06:40:38Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-20T06:40:38Z"}},"author":{"login":"RaviPidaparthi","id":28370364,"profile_url":"https://github.com/RaviPidaparthi","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28370364?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"},"stats":{"additions":507,"deletions":13,"total":520},"files":[{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md","status":"modified","additions":10,"changes":10},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/api/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.net10.0.cs","status":"modified","additions":3,"changes":3},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/api/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.net8.0.cs","status":"modified","additions":3,"changes":3},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/src/Exceptions/ResourceNotFoundException.cs","status":"modified","additions":24,"changes":24},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/src/Internal/ApiErrorFactory.cs","status":"modified","additions":2,"deletions":2,"changes":4},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/src/Internal/ResponseEndpointHandler.cs","status":"modified","additions":10,"changes":10},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/src/Internal/ResponsesExceptionFilter.cs","status":"modified","additions":1,"deletions":1,"changes":2},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/src/Internal/StorageErrorMapper.cs","status":"modified","additions":35,"deletions":10,"changes":45},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Exceptions/ExceptionTypesTests.cs","status":"modified","additions":14,"changes":14},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Internal/ApiErrorFactoryTests.cs","status":"modified","additions":15,"changes":15},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Internal/StorageErrorMapperTests.cs","status":"added","additions":180,"changes":180},{"filename":"sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Protocol/HistoryValidationTests.cs","status":"added","additions":210,"changes":210}]}

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58347 by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed Beta.3 introduced two significant behavioral changes: 1. **Eager validation of `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id`** — the library now resolves these references against the storage provider *before* invoking the handler. Invalid `previous_response_id` returns HTTP 404 immediately; invalid `conversation.id` returns HTTP 400. Previously, these errors were silently ignored. 2. **Richer storage error propagation** — `ResourceNotFoundException` gained two new public properties, `Code` and `Param`, mirroring `BadRequestException`. `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts the full Foundry error body (`code`, `message`, `param`, `type`) and propagates it through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404/409 upstream errors are normalized to HTTP 500 instead of being proxied as-is. ## Gaps Found - The **Troubleshooting** section documents the 404 case only as "The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL." It must also document the new 404 scenario: invalid `previous_response_id` detected during eager validation, and the matching 400 for an invalid `conversation.id`. - The **`ResponseContext`** description for `GetHistoryAsync()` does not mention that history/conversation ID resolution now happens eagerly before the handler is invoked, so handlers will never see a lazy 404 mid-stream from those calls. This is important for handler authors to understand. - There is no documentation (in the README or in `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md`) explaining the new `Code` and `Param` properties on `ResourceNotFoundException`, nor how provider implementers should populate them when throwing this exception. - The README has no mention of the new guarantee that non-4xx upstream storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500, which affects how operators reason about error codes surfaced to clients.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit **A. Update the `ResponseContext` bullet for `GetHistoryAsync`** Locate the bullet: ``` - **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). ``` Replace with: ``` - **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). The library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the provider *before* invoking the handler — invalid references return HTTP 404 or HTTP 400 immediately, and the resolved result is cached so handlers calling `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse it without a second storage lookup. ``` **B. Update the Troubleshooting section** Locate the `### Common errors` section and add two new bullets after the existing 404 entry: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (create): The `previous_response_id` field in the request does not match any existing response. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. - **400 Bad Request** (create): The `conversation.id` field in the request is invalid or references an unknown conversation. Validation occurs before the handler is invoked. ``` Also update the existing entry: ``` - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` to: ``` - **404 Not Found** (get/cancel/delete): The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. ``` **C. Add a note about upstream error normalization in Troubleshooting** After the existing common errors list, add: ```markdown ### Storage error propagation When the library calls Foundry storage and receives a structured error response, it forwards the full error body — `code`, `message`, `param`, and `type` — to the client for 400, 404, and 409 responses. All other upstream error status codes are normalized to HTTP 500 to prevent leaking infrastructure details. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit Find the section that describes exception types thrown from provider implementations (if present). Add documentation for the updated `ResourceNotFoundException`: ```markdown #### ResourceNotFoundException Throw `ResourceNotFoundException` from a provider method when a requested resource (response, input item, etc.) does not exist. The library maps this to HTTP 404. ```csharp throw new ResourceNotFoundException( message: "Response not found.", code: "not_found", // Optional: structured error code forwarded to the client param: "response_id"); // Optional: the field name that caused the error ``` The `Code` and `Param` properties (added in 1.0.0-beta.3) are forwarded verbatim in the HTTP error response body, mirroring the `BadRequestException` API. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these changes — the Troubleshooting and provider exception guidance does not need live code samples. Existing snippets remain valid. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in order: 1. Getting started ✅ (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. Key concepts ✅ 3. Examples ✅ 4. Troubleshooting ✅ — needs the additions described in Step 1 5. Next steps ✅ 6. Contributing ✅ No structural changes are needed; only the content additions described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README changes (no new snippet regions were added) 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes are expected for these doc edits 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [AgentServer] Docs: document eager previous_response_id validation and ResourceNotFoundException.Code/Param
